     The Numbers Business Report

The Jungle Book (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $364,001,123Details
International Box Office $586,696,875
Worldwide Box Office $950,697,998
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $20,468,069
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $26,730,179
Total $47,198,248
Further financial details...

Synopsis

An adventure about Mowgli, a man-cub raised by a family of wolves. But Mowgli finds he is no longer welcome in the jungle when fearsome tiger Shere Khan, who bears the scars of Man, promises to eliminate what he sees as a threat. Urged to abandon the only home he’s ever known, Mowgli embarks on a captivating journey of self-discovery, guided by panther-turned-stern mentor Bagheera, and the free-spirited bear Baloo. Along the way, Mowgli encounters jungle creatures who don’t exactly have his best interests at heart, including Kaa, a python whose seductive voice and gaze hypnotizes the man-cub, and the smooth-talking King Louie, who tries to coerce Mowgli into giving up the secret to the elusive and deadly red flower: fire.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$103,261,464 (28.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.53 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:38.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$175,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,028 opening theaters/4,144 max. theaters, 8.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $503,289,418

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 15th, 2016 (Wide) by Walt Disney
April 15th, 2016 (IMAX) by Walt Disney
International Releases: April 7th, 2016 (Wide) (Bolivia)
April 7th, 2016 (Wide) (Chile)

April 7th, 2016 (Wide) (Argentina)
April 7th, 2016 (Wide) (Malaysia)
Video Release: August 23rd, 2016 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some sequences of scary action and peril.
(Rating bulletin 2410 (Cert #50254), 2/3/2016)
Running Time: 105 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Live Action Remake, Motion Capture Performance, Posthumous Release, Prologue, Remake, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Filming location (city or town): Los Angeles, CA
Filming location (country): United States
Filming location (production facility): Los Angeles Center Studios, Los Angeles, CA
Filming location (state or county): California, United States, Los Angeles County, CA
Geographical setting: India
Plot point: Jungle, Non-Chronological, Orphan, Raised By Wild Animals, Talking Animals, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Time period setting: 1890s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Fantasy
Production/Financing Companies: Walt Disney Pictures, Fairview Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

An adventure about Mowgli, a man-cub raised by a family of wolves. But Mowgli finds he is no longer welcome in the jungle when fearsome tiger Shere Khan, who bears the scars of Man, promises to eliminate what he sees as a threat. Urged to abandon the only home he’s ever known, Mowgli embarks on a captivating journey of self-discovery, guided by panther-turned-stern mentor Bagheera, and the free-spirited bear Baloo. Along the way, Mowgli encounters jungle creatures who don’t exactly have his best interests at heart, including Kaa, a python whose seductive voice and gaze hypnotizes the man-cub, and the smooth-talking King Louie, who tries to coerce Mowgli into giving up the secret to the elusive and deadly red flower: fire.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$103,261,464 (28.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.53 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:38.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$175,000,000 (worldwide box office is 5.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,028 opening theaters/4,144 max. theaters, 8.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $503,289,418

Running Time: 105 minutes

Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
